# \<PROJECT\> Field Data Processing Checklist

**Table of Contents**

[New Project Data Processing Checklist](#new-project-data-processing-checklist)

[SyncThing Configuration](#syncthing-configuration)

[Rclone Configuration](#rclone-configuration)

[Google Drive](#google-drive)

[Ingest location on EOL servers (mercury)](#ingest-location-on-eol-servers-\(mercury\))

[Field Data Tools](#field-data-tools)

## ---

Steps to configure data processing and distribution before a field project

## ---

Before each project, the RAF Ground Station computer, the EOL servers, and a data transfer mechanism must be configured for the project. **SyncThing is the preferred transfer method** (set up by IT); Google Drive (rclone), NAS, and FTP are legacy alternatives. The script init\_project in aircraft\_projects/scripts is the starting point, but there are numerous steps that are necessary thereafter.

## New Project Data Processing Checklist {#new-project-data-processing-checklist}

- [ ] Copy this checklist to the Google drive  “Airborne Field Projects” \-\> \<project\> (May be under 1 \- Active Projects) \-\> Data Management and use the copy rather than this template.  
- [ ] Run init\_project (can be found in proj \-\> scripts)  
- [ ] Run init\_processing (located in $PROJ\_DIR/scripts) on mercury and other servers. Decide on the project name with SEs and PMs. It seems arbitrary, but it can be a headache if a bad project name is used, even internally.  
- [ ] Commit changes.  
- [ ] Pull changes in $PROJ\_DIR on RAF Ground Station. (This is a good opportunity to make sure your ssh git credentials are set up properly. See this wiki page:[https://wiki.ucar.edu/display/SEW/GitSharedAccounts](https://wiki.ucar.edu/display/SEW/GitSharedAccounts))  
- [ ] Update the \~/ads3\_environment.sh file with the new project name on the RAF Ground Station.  
      - [ ] "source \~/ads3\_environment.sh" for changes to take effect.  
- [ ] Update the \~/ads3\_environment.sh file with the new project name on mercury / eol-rosetta.   
      - [ ] "source \~/ads3\_environment.sh" for changes to take effect.  
- [ ] **If using SyncThing (preferred):** Contact IT (current contact: **Ted Russ**) to configure SyncThing for unidirectional syncing from `/var/r1/field_sync` on the RAF Ground Station to the EOL server FTP ingest directory. SyncThing runs as a daemon — no crontab entry is needed on the Ground Station for data transfer.
      - [ ] Confirm the ads user crontab on eol-rosetta contains the sync\_field\_data.py entry to distribute data from the ingest point to data dirs:

| *\# distribute data from SyncThing ingest point to data dirs*\*/10 \* \* \* \* . /h/eol/ads/.bashrc && "$PROJ\_DIR/scripts/sync\_field\_data.py \> /tmp/sync.log 2\>&1" |
| :---- |

- [ ] **If using Google Drive (legacy):** Confirm the ads user crontab on RAF Ground Station contains the rclone entry:

| *\# rclone command to recursively sync the staging dir, to catch files that are added out of sequence with processing using push\_data.py*\*/15 \* \* \* \* . /home/ads/.bashrc && "rclone copy $RAW\_DATA\_DIR/rclone\_staging/$PROJECT gdrive\_eolfield:$PROJECT \--ignore-existing \> /tmp/sync.log 2\>&1" |
| :---- |

- [ ] Confirm the ads user crontab on eol-rosetta contains the rclone entry:

| *\# get data from Google Drive to local ingest point*\*/5 \* \* \* \* . /h/eol/ads/.bashrc && "rclone copy gdrive\_eolfield:$PROJECT $RAW\_DATA\_DIR/$PROJECT/field\_sync \--ignore-existing \> /tmp/sync.log 2\>&1" *\# distribute data from ingest point to data dirs*\*/10 \* \* \* \* . /h/eol/ads/.bashrc && "$PROJ\_DIR/scripts/sync\_field\_data.py \> /tmp/sync.log 2\>&1" |
| :---- |

- [ ] If the project has AVAPS (dropsonde), the following crontab entries may be necessary. Work with the AVAPS group to determine their needs:

| *\# get data from local ingest point and write to Google drive \- supports dropsonde processing*\*/5 \* \* \* \* . /h/eol/ads/.bashrc && "rclone copy $RAW\_DATA\_DIR/$PROJECT/field\_sync gdrive\_eolfield:$PROJECT \--ignore-existing \> /tmp/sync.log 2\>&1" *\# Copy AVAPS files from rdat to ftp area (last used for CAESAR)*\*/4 \* \* \* \* . /h/eol/ads/.bashrc && "rclone copy $RAW\_DATA\_DIR/$PROJECT/field\_sync/EOL\_data/AVAPS\_data /net/ftp/pub/data/incoming/$PROJECT/EOL\_data/AVAPS\_data \--ignore-existing \> /tmp/sync.log 2\>&1" |
| :---- |

- [ ] Email help@ucar.edu and ask to have the incoming ftp directory /net/ftp/pub/data/incoming/\<project\> (where project is lower case) created on the server with the username being the project in lower case and password ideally being the same one used for the field catalog \- talk to the catalog lead to coordinate. The catalog lead should be listed in the project-specific copy of the  [Field Data Management Requirements](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1n6jQDekYRBMcgsNZzNP3moeMOrll84sHN1duaVgir3s/edit#gid=1973283384) document under the RAF.  
- [ ] If you are not already, ask to be a member of the ftp group on mercury (only mercury) so you can rw files since it's g+w to the ftp group.   
      - [ ] then create the tree below it:

| EOL\_data/    RAF\_data/PI\_data/ |
| :---- |

- [ ] Ensure the following dirs exist (*create if needed*)
      - [ ] Ground Station Data Directory: $RAW\_DATA\_DIR/$PROJECT
      - [ ] **If using SyncThing:** Ground Station SyncThing Staging: `/var/r1/field_sync/EOL_Data/RAF_Data` with subdirs for each data type (LRT, HRT, SRT, ADS, KML, QA\_Tools, etc.)
      - [ ] **If using Google Drive:** Ground Station Rclone Staging: $RAW\_DATA\_DIR/rclone\_staging/$PROJECT
      - [ ] **If using Google Drive:** Google Drive: EOL\_Field\_Data/\<PROJECT\>/EOL\_data/RAF\_data/LRT, etc.
      - [ ] Mercury Ingest Directory: $RAW\_DATA\_DIR/$PROJECT
      - [ ] Mercury Dat: $DATA\_DIR/$PROJECT, $DATA\_DIR/$PROJECT/field\_data
- [ ] Configure `fieldProc_setup.py` to create the data products required for the project (located at `$PROJ_DIR/$PROJECT/$AIRCRAFT/scripts/`). A template is available at `$PROJ_DIR/Configuration/scripts/fieldProc_setup.py`.
      - [ ] **If using SyncThing:** set `SYNCTHING = True` and `syncthing_staging_dir = f'/var/r1/field_sync/EOL_Data/RAF_Data'`
      - [ ] **If using Google Drive:** set `GDRIVE = True`. Update the [rclone configuration](#rclone-configuration) as described below.
- [ ] Make sure that fieldProc\_setup.py is in the PATH and PYTHONPATH. If necessary, update the export PATH and export PYTHONPATH lines in the .bashrc file, wherever you are running.  
- [ ] Link the fieldProc\_setup.py file to the desktop for easy access  
- [ ] Clone the aircraft\_QAtools\_notebook at /home/local. If already cloned, perform a git pull.  
      - [ ] Configure [QAtools\_notebook](https://github.com/NCAR/aircraft_QAtools_notebook/blob/main/README.md) (if not done when configuring RAFGroundstation)  
- [ ] Clone [https://github.com/NCAR/kml\_tools](https://github.com/NCAR/kml_tools) at /home/local and build acTrack2kml using scons)  
- [ ] Make sure that conda environments are set up correctly. There is an environment.yml file in these locations: /home/local/nc\_utils and /home/local/aircraft\_QAtools\_notebook, however the base conda environment should have everything needed for running aircraft\_QAtools\_notebook and nc\_utils. See this wiki page for more details on conda environments: [https://wiki.ucar.edu/display/SEW/Anaconda+Python+in+EOL](https://wiki.ucar.edu/display/SEW/Anaconda+Python+in+EOL)  
- [ ] If email notification via SMTP is failing (\[Errno 111\] Connection refused), install and start postfix as root:

| dnf install postfix \-ysystemctl start postfixsystemctl enable postfix |
| :---- |

- [ ] Be sure to delete any test data before actual project flights begin to avoid confusion.  
      

### SyncThing Configuration {#syncthing-configuration}

[SyncThing](https://syncthing.net/) is the preferred method for transferring field data from the RAF Ground Station to EOL servers. It is configured and managed by IT — the current contact is **Ted Russ**.

SyncThing is set up for **unidirectional syncing** from `/var/r1/field_sync` on the RAF Ground Station (`gs3`) to the EOL server FTP ingest directory. Once configured, it runs as a background daemon and requires no crontab entries or manual intervention for data transfer.

**Setup steps:**

1. Contact IT (Ted Russ) to configure the SyncThing share for the new project before the project begins.
2. In `fieldProc_setup.py`, set:
   ```
   SYNCTHING = True
   syncthing_staging_dir = f'/var/r1/field_sync/EOL_Data/RAF_Data'
   ```
3. Ensure the staging directory and subdirectories exist on the Ground Station (`LRT/`, `HRT/`, `SRT/`, `ADS/`, `KML/`, `QA_Tools/`, etc.).
4. On eol-rosetta, confirm `sync_field_data.py` is running as a crontab to distribute data from the FTP ingest point to the appropriate data directories. When `SYNCTHING = True`, `sync_field_data.py` uses the same FTP-based distribution logic internally, since SyncThing delivers files to the same FTP ingest location on the EOL servers.

### Rclone Configuration {#rclone-configuration}

rclone ( [https://rclone.org/](https://rclone.org/) ) is installed and configured on the RAF Ground Station Computer and on mercury.

To enter configuration, type rclone config in a terminal.

| (base) 10003% rclone config Current remotes: Name                 Type \====                 \==== gdrive\_eolfield      drive e) Edit existing remote n) New remote d) Delete remote r) Rename remote c) Copy remote s) Set configuration password q) Quit config e/n/d/r/c/s/q\>  |
| :---- |

The remote gdrive\_eolfield is currently configured for both RAF Ground Station computers and mercury. A client\_id and client\_secret are stored, to ensure higher performance during data transfer. rclone documentation notes that if you use a default client\_id, you share across other rclone users who are also using the default client\_id. Instructions for generating a client\_id are here: [https://rclone.org/drive/\#making-your-own-client-id](https://rclone.org/drive/#making-your-own-client-id) . The same client\_id and client\_secret are used for the RAF Ground Station and the EOL server.

The credential information, including the client\_id and client secret, can be found here:  
[https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/api/drive.googleapis.com/credentials?project=eol-field-data\&supportedpurview=project](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/api/drive.googleapis.com/credentials?project=eol-field-data&supportedpurview=project) if needed you can create a new credential there.

The EOL-Field-Data project can be accessed here: [https://console.cloud.google.com/home/dashboard?project=eol-field-data\&supportedpurview=project](https://console.cloud.google.com/home/dashboard?project=eol-field-data&supportedpurview=project) 

Command to copy from source to destination:

rclone copy \<source\> \<destination\> \--ignore-existing 

Example:

| rclone copy gdrive\_eolfield:CGWAVES /scr/raf/Raw\_Data/CGWAVES/field\_sync \--ignore-existing |
| :---- |

**It is important to use the flag \--ignore-existing because this ensures that any file deletions on local do not propagate to remote.**

### Google Drive {#google-drive}

The Google Drive shared folder [EOL\_Field\_Data](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/0AGSDsH7HTW3UUk9PVA) contains a folder for each project that is the name of the project, and it contains two subdirectores.

1. The first, EOL\_data, is for EOL datasets that are generated in the field. These files are generated on the RAF Ground Station computer, where they are copied to a staging location. rclone is used to copy to the remote Google Drive shared folder, which is configured before each project.   
2. The second, PI\_data, is for PI data that are generated in the field.

## Ingest location on EOL servers (mercury) {#ingest-location-on-eol-servers-(mercury)}

The files placed in the project folder are copied to an ingest location `/scr/raf/Raw_Data/<PROJECT>/field_sync` on the EOL servers — either via SyncThing (preferred) or via rclone from Google Drive (legacy). All directory structure is maintained. From this ingest location, `sync_field_data.py` distributes the appropriate files to `/scr/raf_data/<PROJECT>/field_data`, `/scr/raf_data/<PROJECT>`, and `/scr/raf/Raw_Data/<PROJECT>`. `sync_field_data.py` is located at:

| /net/jlocal/projects/scripts/sync\_field\_data.py  |
| :---- |

`sync_field_data.py` is executed as a crontab on eol-rosetta. When using SyncThing, no additional rclone crontab is needed — SyncThing handles the transfer automatically. The current crontab settings are in the checklist at the top of this document.

## Field Data Tools {#field-data-tools}

Ongoing updates need in advance of every project (apart from creating directory structures):

- [ ] push\_data.py
- [ ] run\_push\_data.sh: copies raw data files from a physical drive to the Ground Station data directory. Run from `scripts/data_flow/` with an optional destination argument (defaults to `/var/r1/$PROJECT`). Used when raw ADS data arrives on a physical drive rather than being written directly to the data directory.
- [ ] [fieldProc\_setup.py: update based on project information](https://wiki.ucar.edu/display/SEW/RAFGroundstation)
      - [ ] Set `SYNCTHING = True` and `GDRIVE = False` for the standard SyncThing workflow.
      - [ ] Set `GDRIVE = True` and `SYNCTHING = False` to use Google Drive (legacy).
      - [ ] Legacy Configuration with NAS and FTP: If you wish to configure the data flow to use NAS and/or FTP, set the corresponding booleans. GDRIVE and SYNCTHING should be set to False in this case.
- [ ] sync\_field\_data.py  
- [ ] nc2asc.bat: updated variable list if ICARTT is desired.  
- [ ] aircraft\_QAtools\_notebook  
- [ ] We now have a template for [Field Data Management Requirements](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1n6jQDekYRBMcgsNZzNP3moeMOrll84sHN1duaVgir3s/edit#gid=1973283384) that helps walk us through everything we need to set up data management for a project.
